member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_NCRENDERING_ENABLED
member.summary: Use with DwmGetWindowAttribute. Discovers whether non-client rendering is enabled. The retrieved value is of type BOOL. TRUE if non-client rendering is enabled; otherwise, FALSE.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_NCRENDERING_POLICY
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Sets the non-client rendering policy. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value from the DWMNCRENDERINGPOLICY enumeration.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_TRANSITIONS_FORCEDISABLED
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Enables or forcibly disables DWM transitions. T he pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to disable transitions, or FALSE to enable transitions.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_ALLOW_NCPAINT
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Enables content rendered in the non-client area to be visible on the frame drawn by DWM. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to enable content rendered in the non-client area to be visible on the frame; otherwise, FALSE.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_CAPTION_BUTTON_BOUNDS
member.summary: Use with DwmGetWindowAttribute. Retrieves the bounds of the caption button area in the window-relative space. The retrieved value is of type RECT. If the window is minimized or otherwise not visible to the user, then the value of the RECT retrieved is undefined. You should check whether the retrieved RECT contains a boundary that you can work with, and if it doesn't then you can conclude that the window is minimized or otherwise not visible.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_NONCLIENT_RTL_LAYOUT
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Specifies whether non-client content is right-to-left (RTL) mirrored. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE if the non-client content is right-to-left (RTL) mirrored; otherwise, FALSE.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_FORCE_ICONIC_REPRESENTATION
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Forces the window to display an iconic thumbnail or peek representation (a static bitmap), even if a live or snapshot representation of the window is available. This value is normally set during a window's creation, and not changed throughout the window's lifetime. Some scenarios, however, might require the value to change over time. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to require a iconic thumbnail or peek representation; otherwise, FALSE.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_FLIP3D_POLICY
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Sets how Flip3D treats the window. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value from the DWMFLIP3DWINDOWPOLICY enumeration.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_EXTENDED_FRAME_BOUNDS
member.summary: Use with DwmGetWindowAttribute. Retrieves the extended frame bounds rectangle in screen space. The retrieved value is of type RECT.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_HAS_ICONIC_BITMAP
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. The window will provide a bitmap for use by DWM as an iconic thumbnail or peek representation (a static bitmap) for the window. DWMWA_HAS_ICONIC_BITMAP can be specified with DWMWA_FORCE_ICONIC_REPRESENTATION. DWMWA_HAS_ICONIC_BITMAP normally is set during a window's creation and not changed throughout the window's lifetime. Some scenarios, however, might require the value to change over time. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to inform DWM that the window will provide an iconic thumbnail or peek representation; otherwise, FALSE.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_DISALLOW_PEEK
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Do not show peek preview for the window. The peek view shows a full-sized preview of the window when the mouse hovers over the window's thumbnail in the taskbar. If this attribute is set, hovering the mouse pointer over the window's thumbnail dismisses peek (in case another window in the group has a peek preview showing). The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to prevent peek functionality, or FALSE to allow it.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_EXCLUDED_FROM_PEEK
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Prevents a window from fading to a glass sheet when peek is invoked. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to prevent the window from fading during another window's peek, or FALSE for normal behavior.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_CLOAK
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Cloaks the window such that it is not visible to the user. The window is still composed by DWM. Using with DirectComposition: Use the DWMWA_CLOAK flag to cloak the layered child window when animating a representation of the window's content via a DirectComposition visual that has been associated with the layered child window. For more details on this usage case, see How to animate the bitmap of a layered child window.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_CLOAKED
member.summary: Use with DwmGetWindowAttribute. If the window is cloaked, provides one of the following values explaining why. DWM_CLOAKED_APP (value 0x0000001). The window was cloaked by its owner application. DWM_CLOAKED_SHELL (value 0x0000002). The window was cloaked by the Shell. DWM_CLOAKED_INHERITED (value 0x0000004). The cloak value was inherited from its owner window.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_FREEZE_REPRESENTATION
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Freeze the window's thumbnail image with its current visuals. Do no further live updates on the thumbnail image to match the window's contents.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_USE_HOSTBACKDROPBRUSH
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Enables a non-UWP window to use host backdrop brushes. If this flag is set, then a Win32 app that calls Windows::UI::Composition APIs can build transparency effects using the host backdrop brush (see Compositor.CreateHostBackdropBrush). The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to enable host backdrop brushes for the window, or FALSE to disable it. Value is supported with Windows 11 Build 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_USE_IMMERSIVE_DARK_MODE
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Allows the window frame for this window to be drawn in dark mode colors when the dark mode system setting is enabled. For compatibility reasons, all windows default to light mode regardless of the system setting. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type BOOL. TRUE to honor dark mode for the window, FALSE to always use light mode. Windows 11 Build 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_WINDOW_CORNERPREFERENCE
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Specifies the rounded corner preference for a window. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type DWM_WINDOW_CORNER_PREFERENCE. Windows 11 Build 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_BORDER_COLOR
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Specifies the color of the window border. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type COLORREF. The app is responsible for changing the border color according to state changes, such as a change in window activation. Windows 11 Build 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_CAPTION_COLOR
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Specifies the color of the caption. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type COLORREF. Windows 11 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_TEXT_COLOR
member.summary: Use with DwmSetWindowAttribute. Specifies the color of the caption text. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type COLORREF. Windows 11 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_VISIBLE_FRAME_BORDER_THICKNESS
member.summary: Use with DwmGetWindowAttribute. Retrieves the width of the outer border that the DWM would draw around this window. The value can vary depending on the DPI of the window. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type UINT. Windows 11 22000

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Interop.Win32.DWMWINDOWATTRIBUTE.DWMWA_SYSTEMBACKDROP_TYPE
member.summary: Use with DwmGetWindowAttribute or DwmSetWindowAttribute. Retrieves or specifies the system-drawn backdrop material of a window, including behind the non-client area. The pvAttribute parameter points to a value of type DWM_SYSTEMBACKDROP_TYPE. Windows 11 22621

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme
member.summary: Theme manager for FluentAvalonia, managing various components of the Fluentv2 theme like AccentColor, styles, and platform settings

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.#ctor
member.summary: Create new instance of .

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.HighContrastTheme
member.summary: High Contrast Theme

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.UseSystemFontOnWindows
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the system font should be used on Windows. Value only applies at startup
member.remarks: On Windows 10, this is "Segoe UI", and Windows 11, this is "Segoe UI Variable Text".

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.PreferSystemTheme
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to use the current system theme (light or dark mode).
member.remarks: This property is respected on Windows, MacOS, and Linux. However, on linux, the detection is different depending on the user's desktop environment. On KDE, Cinnamon, LXDE and LXQt, it requires the user's theme (color scheme in the case of KDE) name to contain "dark". On GNOME or Xfce, it requires 'color-scheme' to be set to either 'prefer-light', 'prefer-dark', or 'gtk-theme' to contain 'dark'. Also note, that high contrast theme will only resolve here on Windows.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.PreferUserAccentColor
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to use the current user's accent color as the resource SystemAccentColor
member.remarks: On Linux, accent color detection is only supported on KDE (from current scheme, from wallpaper and custom), LXQt (from selection color) and LXDE (from custom selection color).

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.CustomAccentColor
member.summary: Gets or sets a to use as the SystemAccentColor for the app. Note this takes precedence over the property and must be set to null to restore the system color, if desired
member.remarks: The 6 variants (3 light/3 dark) are pregenerated from the given color. FluentAvalonia makes no checks to ensure the legibility and accessibility of the chosen color and places that responsibility upon you. For more control over the accent color variants, directly override SystemAccentColor or the variants in the Application level resource dictionary.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.TextVerticalAlignmentOverrideBehavior
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that determines if/when style overrides should be used to alleviate issues with text alignment in some controls caused when Segoe UI or Segoe UI Variable font families do not exist. The default value is
member.remarks: These overrides apply to controls like RadioButton, CheckBox, ComboBox where the first line of text is explicitly aligned with the control. Adding the overrides modify the styles to use VerticalAlignment=Center to get a consistent experience, at the (small) expense of breaking Fluent design principles. If your controls never use multi-line text, you'll never see the effect of this property.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Styling.FluentAvaloniaTheme.ForceWin32WindowToTheme(Avalonia.Controls.Window,Avalonia.Styling.ThemeVariant)
member.summary: On Windows, forces a specific to the current theme
member.param: The theme to use, or null to use the current RequestedTheme

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.Styling.TextVerticalAlignmentOverride
member.summary: Specifies constants for when text alignment style overrides should be applied See and GH PR #210

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Styling.TextVerticalAlignmentOverride.Disabled
member.summary: Specified no text alignment overrides should be used
member.remarks: This value should be used if your app is Windows only or if you know the Segoe font family is installed (or you don't care about the text alignment issues)

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Styling.TextVerticalAlignmentOverride.EnabledNonWindows
member.summary: Specifies text alignment style overrides should be applied only on non-Windows operating systems
member.remarks: This value should be used if you don't care about potential styling differences between Windows vs non-Windows and want to maintain Fluent design principles on Windows.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.Styling.TextVerticalAlignmentOverride.AlwaysEnabled
member.summary: Specifies text alignment style overrides should always be applied
member.remarks: This value should be used if you want to ensure the same look across operating systems

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame
member.summary: Displays instances (Pages in WinUI), supports navigation to new pages, and maintains a navigation history to support forward and backward navigation.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.GoBack
member.summary: Navigates to the most recent item in back navigation history, if a Frame manages its own navigation history.

member.summary: Navigates to the most recent item in back navigation history, if a Frame manages its own navigation history, and specifies the animated transition to use.
member.param: Info about the animated transition to use.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.GoForward
member.summary: Navigates to the most recent item in forward navigation history, if a Frame manages its own navigation history.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.Navigate(System.Type)
member.summary: Causes the Frame to load content represented by the specified Page.
member.param: The page (IControl) to navigate to, specified as a type reference to its class type, or if a this can be any type (e.g., a ViewModel)
member.returns: false if a event handler has set Handled to true; otherwise, true.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.Navigate(System.Type,System.Object)
member.summary: Causes the Frame to load content represented by the specified Page, also passing a parameter to be interpreted by the target of the navigation.
member.param: The page (IControl) to navigate to, specified as a type reference to its class type, or if a this can be any type (e.g., a ViewModel)
member.returns: false if a event handler has set Handled to true; otherwise, true.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.Navigate(System.Type,System.Object,FluentAvalonia.UI.Media.Animation.NavigationTransitionInfo)
member.summary: Causes the Frame to load content represented by the specified Page -derived data type, also passing a parameter to be interpreted by the target of the navigation, and a value indicating the animated transition to use.
member.param: The page (IControl) to navigate to, specified as a type reference to its class type, or if a this can be any type (e.g., a ViewModel)
The navigation parameter to pass to the target page; must have a basic type (string, char, numeric, or GUID) to support parameter serialization using GetNavigationState.
member.returns: false if a event handler has set Handled to true; otherwise, true.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.NavigateToType(System.Type,System.Object,FluentAvalonia.UI.Navigation.FrameNavigationOptions)
member.summary: Causes the Frame to load content represented by the specified Page, also passing a parameter to be interpreted by the target of the navigation.
member.param: The page (IControl) to navigate to, specified as a type reference to its class type, or if a this can be any type (e.g., a ViewModel)
The navigation parameter to pass to the target page; must have a basic type (string, char, numeric, or GUID) to support parameter serialization using GetNavigationState.
Options for the navigation, including whether it is recorded in the navigation stack and what transition animation is used.
member.returns: false if a event handler has set Handled to true; otherwise, true.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.NavigateFromObject(System.Object,FluentAvalonia.UI.Navigation.FrameNavigationOptions)
member.summary: Causes the frame to load content represented by the specified target property with the specified navigation options
member.remarks: You must specify a for this method to succeed
member.param: An existing object for which page creation should be based (e.g., A ViewModel instance)
Options for the navigation, including whether it is recorded in the navigation stack and what transition animation is used.
member.returns: false if a event handler has set Handled to true or if is not specified; otherwise, true.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.GetNavigationState
member.summary: Serializes the Frame navigation history into a string

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.SetNavigationState(System.String)
member.summary: Reads and restores the navigation history of a Frame from a provided serialization string.
member.param: The serialization string that supplies the restore point for navigation history.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Frame.SetNavigationState(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Reads and restores the navigation history of a Frame from a provided serialization string, and optionally supresses navigation to the last page type
member.param: The serialization string that supplies the restore point for navigation history.
true to restore navigation history without navigating to the current page; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Calling SetNavigationState with suppressNavigate set to true, OnNavigatedTo is not called and the current page is placed into the BackStack

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.InfoBarSeverity.Informational
member.summary: Communicates that the InfoBar is displaying general information that requires the user's attention. For assistive technologies, they will follow the behavior set in the Processing_All constant.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.InfoBarSeverity.Success
member.summary: Communicates that the InfoBar is displaying information regarding a long-running and/or background task that has completed successfully. For assistive technologies, they will follow the behavior set in the Processing_All constant.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.InfoBarSeverity.Warning
member.summary: Communicates that the InfoBar is displaying information regarding a condition that might cause a problem in the future. For assistive technologies, they will follow the behavior set in the NotificationProcessing_ImportantAll constan

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.InfoBarSeverity.Error
member.summary: Communicates that the InfoBar is displaying information regarding an error or problem that has occurred. For assistive technologies, they will follow the behavior set in the NotificationProcessing_ImportantAll constant.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewDisplayMode
member.summary: Defines constants that specify how the pane is shown in a NavigationView.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewDisplayMode.Minimal
member.summary: Only the menu button remains fixed. The pane shows and hides as needed.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewDisplayMode.Compact
member.summary: The pane always shows as a narrow sliver which can be opened to full width.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewDisplayMode.Expanded
member.summary: The pane stays open alongside the content.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode
member.summary: Defines constants that specify how and where the NavigationView pane is shown.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode.Auto
member.summary: The pane is shown on the left side of the control, and changes between minimal, compact, and full states depending on the width of the window.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode.Left
member.summary: The pane is shown on the left side of the control in its fully open state.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode.Top
member.summary: The pane is shown at the top of the control.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode.LeftCompact
member.summary: The pane is shown on the left side of the control. Only the pane icons are shown by default.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode.LeftMinimal
member.summary: The pane is shown on the left side of the control. Only the pane menu button is shown by default.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.SelectionNode
member.summary: Tracks nested selection.
member.remarks: SelectionNode is the internal tree data structure that we keep track of for selection in a nested scenario. This would map to one ItemsSourceView/Collection. This node reacts to collection changes and keeps the selected indices up to date. This can either be a leaf node or a non leaf node.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.NumberBox.SimpleNumberFormat
member.summary: Use this for simple number formatting using normal .net formatting. Resulting string must still be numeric in value, no special characters, as they are not removed when attempting to convert text to value
member.remarks: This property cannot be used if is also in use

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.RangeSlider.MinimumRange
member.summary: Gets or sets the smallest acceptable range between and when dragging the thumb
member.remarks: Use this property to set a minimum distance (in data units) the slider thumbs can get during a drag operation to prevent them from overlapping. NOTE: This property does NOT have any effect if the RangeStart or RangeEnd is set programmatically, i.e., Start = 30, End = 50, MinimumRange=15, you cannot drag the RangeStart thumb to 40, but you can still programmatically set RangeStart to 40.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout
member.summary: Represents the base class for an object that sizes and arranges child elements for a host.

member.name: E:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.MeasureInvalidated
member.summary: Occurs when the measurement state (layout) has been invalidated.

member.name: E:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.ArrangeInvalidated
member.summary: Occurs when the arrange state(layout) has been invalidated.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.InitializeForContext(FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.LayoutContext)
member.summary: Initializes any per-container state the layout requires when it is attached to a UIElement container.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.UninitializeForContext(FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.LayoutContext)
member.summary: Removes any state the layout previously stored on the UIElement container.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.Measure(FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.LayoutContext,Avalonia.Size)
member.summary: Suggests a DesiredSize for a container element. A container element that supports attached layouts should call this method from their own MeasureOverride implementations to form a recursive layout update. The attached layout is expected to call the Measure for each of the container’s UIElement children.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.Arrange(FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.LayoutContext,Avalonia.Size)
member.summary: Positions child elements and determines a size for a container UIElement. Container elements that support attached layouts should call this method from their layout override implementations to form a recursive layout update.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.InvalidateMeasure
member.summary: Invalidates the measurement state (layout) for all UIElement containers that reference this layout.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.Layout.InvalidateArrange
member.summary: Invalidates the arrange state (layout) for all UIElement containers that reference this layout. After the invalidation, the UIElement will have its layout updated, which occurs asynchronously.

member.summary: Gets or sets an object that represents the state of a layout.

member.summary: Represents the base class for an object that sizes and arranges child elements for a host and and does not support virtualization.
member.remarks: NonVirtualizingLayout is the base class for layouts that do not support virtualization. You can inherit from it to create your own layout. A non-virtualizing layout can measure and arrange child elements.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.Orientation
member.summary: Gets or sets the axis along which items are laid out.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.MinItemWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum width of each item.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.MinItemHeight
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum height of each item.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.MinRowSpacing
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum space between items on the vertical axis.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.MinColumnSpacing
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum space between items on the horizontal axis.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.ItemsJustification
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates how items are aligned on the non-scrolling or non-virtualizing axis.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.ItemsStretch
member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates how items are sized to fill the available space.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayout.MaximumRowsOrColumns
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of items rendered per row or column, based on the orientation of the UniformGridLayout.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification
member.summary: Defines constants that specify how items are aligned on the non-scrolling or non-virtualizing axis.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification.Start
member.summary: Items are aligned with the start of the row or column, with extra space at the end. Spacing between items does not change.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification.Center
member.summary: Items are aligned in the center of the row or column, with extra space at the start and end. Spacing between items does not change.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification.End
member.summary: Items are aligned with the end of the row or column, with extra space at the start. Spacing between items does not change.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification.SpaceAround
member.summary: Items are aligned so that extra space is added evenly before and after each item.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification.SpaceBetween
member.summary: Items are aligned so that extra space is added evenly between adjacent items. No space is added at the start or end.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsJustification.SpaceEvenly
member.summary: Items are aligned so that extra space is added evenly before and after each item.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsStretch
member.summary: Defines constants that specify how items are sized to fill the available space in a UniformGridLayout.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsStretch.None
member.summary: The item retains its natural size. Use of extra space is determined by the ItemsJustification property.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsStretch.Fill
member.summary: The item is sized to fill the available space in the non-scrolling direction. Item size in the scrolling direction is not changed.

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.UniformGridLayoutItemsStretch.Uniform
member.summary: The item is sized to both fill the available space in the non-scrolling direction and maintain its aspect ratio.

member.summary: Defines constants that describe the behavior of the close button contained with each TabViewItem

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode.Auto
member.summary: Behavior is defined by the framework. Default: this always maps to Always

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode.OnPointerOver
member.summary: The selected tab always shows the close button if it is closable. Unselected tabs show the close button when the tab is closable and the user has their pointer over the tab

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode.Always
member.summary: The selected tab always show the close button. Unselected tabs always show the close button if they are closable

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewWidthMode
member.summary: Defines constants that specify the width of the tab

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewWidthMode.Equal
member.summary: Each tab has the same width

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewWidthMode.SizeToContent
member.summary: Each tab adjusts its width to the content within the tab

member.name: F:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TabViewWidthMode.Compact
member.summary: Unselected tabs collapse to show only their icon. The selected tab adjusts to display the content within the tab

member.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the teaching tip will constrain to the bounds of its xaml root.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TeachingTip.IsLightDismissEnabled
member.summary: Enables light-dismiss functionality so that a teaching tip will dismiss when a user scrolls or interacts with other elements of the application.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TeachingTip.PreferredPlacement
member.summary: Preferred placement to be used for the teaching tip. If there is not enough space to show at the preferred placement, a new placement will be automatically chosen. Placement is relative to its target if Target is non-null or to the parent window of the teaching tip if Target is null.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TeachingTip.HeroContentPlacement
member.summary: Placement of the hero content within the teaching tip.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TeachingTip.HeroContent
member.summary: Border-to-border graphic content displayed in the header or footer of the teaching tip. Will appear opposite of the tail in targeted teaching tips unless otherwise set.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TeachingTip.IconSource
member.summary: Gets or sets the graphic content to appear alongside the title and subtitle.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Controls.TeachingTip.TemplateSettings

member.summary: Called by AppWindow to run necessary background tasks during the splashscreen

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.UI.Windowing.IApplicationSplashScreen.MinimumShowTime
member.summary: Specifies the minimum show time (in milliseconds) for the SplashScreen.
member.remarks: For quick background loading jobs, you may get undesirable visual effects from the window opening, and immediately switching from Splash to main content. If the background tasks (i.e., RunTasks()) finishes before this time, the background thread will hold until the desired time elapses, before returning to let AppWindow finish opening.

member.name: T:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1
member.summary: Implements a variable-size list that uses a pooled array to store the elements. A PooledList has a capacity, which is the allocated length of the internal array. As elements are added to a PooledList, the capacity of the PooledList is automatically increased as required by reallocating the internal array.
member.remarks: This class is based on the code for but it supports and uses when allocating internal arrays.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.#ctor
member.summary: Constructs a PooledList. The list is initially empty and has a capacity of zero. Upon adding the first element to the list the capacity is increased to DefaultCapacity, and then increased in multiples of two as required.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.Capacity
member.summary: Gets and sets the capacity of this list. The capacity is the size of the internal array used to hold items. When set, the internal Memory of the list is reallocated to the given capacity. Note that the return value for this property may be larger than the property was set to.

member.name: P:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.ClearMode
member.summary: Returns the ClearMode behavior for the collection, denoting whether values are cleared from internal arrays before returning them to the pool.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.AddRange(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Adds the elements of the given collection to the end of this list. If required, the capacity of the list is increased to twice the previous capacity or the new size, whichever is larger.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.AddRange(`0[])

member.summary: Advances the by the number of items specified, increasing the capacity if required, then returns a Span representing the set of items to be added, allowing direct writes to that section of the collection.
member.param: The number of items to add.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.AsSpan

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.BinarySearch(System.Int32,System.Int32,`0,System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{`0})
member.summary: Searches a section of the list for a given element using a binary search algorithm.

remarks.para: Elements of the list are compared to the search value using the given IComparer interface. If comparer is null, elements of the list are compared to the search value using the IComparable interface, which in that case must be implemented by all elements of the list and the given search value. This method assumes that the given section of the list is already sorted; if this is not the case, the result will be incorrect.

The method returns the index of the given value in the list. If the list does not contain the given value, the method returns a negative integer. The bitwise complement operator (~) can be applied to a negative result to produce the index of the first element (if any) that is larger than the given search value. This is also the index at which the search value should be inserted into the list in order for the list to remain sorted.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.BinarySearch(`0)
member.summary: Searches the list for a given element using a binary search algorithm. If the item implements then that is used for comparison, otherwise is used.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.BinarySearch(`0,System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{`0})
member.summary: Searches the list for a given element using a binary search algorithm. If the item implements then that is used for comparison, otherwise is used.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.EnsureCapacity(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures that the capacity of this list is at least the given minimum value. If the current capacity of the list is less than min, the capacity is increased to twice the current capacity or to min, whichever is larger.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Returns an enumerator for this list with the given permission for removal of elements. If modifications made to the list while an enumeration is in progress, the MoveNext and GetObject methods of the enumerator will throw an exception.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.IndexOf(`0,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the index of the first occurrence of a given value in a range of this list. The list is searched forwards, starting at index index and ending at count number of elements.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.IndexOf(`0,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns the index of the first occurrence of a given value in a range of this list. The list is searched forwards, starting at index index and upto count number of elements.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.Reverse(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Reverses the elements in a range of this list. Following a call to this method, an element in the range given by index and count which was previously located at index i will now be located at index + (index + count - i - 1).

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.Sort
member.summary: Sorts the elements in this list. Uses the default comparer and Array.Sort.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.Sort(System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{`0})
member.summary: Sorts the elements in this list. Uses Array.Sort with the provided comparer.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.Sort(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.IComparer{`0})
member.summary:
Sorts the elements in a section of this list. The sort compares the elements to each other using the given IComparer interface. If comparer is null, the elements are compared to each other using the IComparable interface, which in that case must be implemented by all elements of the list.
This method uses the Array.Sort method to sort the elements.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.ToArray
member.summary: ToArray returns an array containing the contents of the List. This requires copying the List, which is an O(n) operation.

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.TrimExcess
member.summary:
Sets the capacity of this list to the size of the list. This method can be used to minimize a list's memory overhead once it is known that no new elements will be added to the list. To completely clear a list and release all memory referenced by the list, execute the following statements:
list.Clear(); list.TrimExcess();

member.name: M:FluentAvalonia.Collections.PooledList`1.Dispose
member.summary: Returns the internal buffers to the ArrayPool.
